title: TVAR Definition Schema
description: Defines a TVAR (Tunable VARiable) following TVL 0.9 specification. A TVAR is a knob, parameter, or design choice whose value Traigent explores during optimization.

TVARType.description:
Variable type:
- bool: Boolean toggle
- int: Integer value
- float: Floating-point value
- str: Free-form string
- enum: One of a fixed set of values

TVARDomain.description: Domain specification defining the range of valid values for a TVAR

values.description: Allowed values (for enum and str types)

range.description: [min, max] range (for int and float types)

resolution.description: Step size for numeric types (defines discretization granularity)

name.description: Variable name (must be valid Python identifier)

domain.description: Valid value domain for this TVAR

default.description: Default value (type depends on TVAR type)

scale.description: Sampling scale for numeric types. Use 'log' for parameters like learning_rate where logarithmic exploration is appropriate.

agent.description: Agent name for multi-agent grouping. Groups TVARs by the agent or component they belong to.

is_tool.description: True if this TVAR configures an MCP tool

constraints.description: Conditional constraints on this TVAR (e.g., 'requires model == gpt-4')
